senses.definition: a child who had parents that were not married to one another

examples.x: Despite not being married, Tom and Jane welcomed a beautiful love child into their lives.
The actress had a love child with her co-star.

senses.definition: a woman that is regarded as the one who provides for her family

word_list.word: born on the wrong side of the blanket
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɔːɹn ɑːnðə ɹˈɔŋ sˈaɪd ʌvðə blˈæŋkɪt

senses.definition: used to refer to a child whose parents were not married at the time of birth

examples.x: Despite being born on the wrong side of the blanket, Sarah grew up to become a successful entrepreneur and proved that one's origins don't define their potential.
John's family disowned him when they discovered he was born on the wrong side of the blanket, but he found solace and acceptance among friends who appreciated him for who he was.

senses.definition: used to refer to a place one was born or grew up in

examples.x: As a born and bred Texan, Mark has a deep appreciation for country music and barbecues.
Sandra is a true New Yorker, born and bred in the heart of the city.
After months of anticipation, the couple announced that they have a bun in the oven.
Last year, Monica had a bun in the oven and gave birth to a beautiful baby girl.

word_list.pronunciation: ɹˈæp ˌɛsbˈiː ˌʌp ɪn kˈɑːʔn̩ wˈʊl

senses.definition: to protect or treat someone or something in an overly cautious or overprotective manner

examples.x: Lily's parents wrapped her up in cotton wool, ensuring she was safe from any potential danger.
Growing up in a small, sheltered town, he felt like the community was trying to wrap him up in cotton wool, preventing him from experiencing the real world.
The company's strict policies seemed to wrap employees up in cotton wool, limiting their ability to make decisions and take calculated risks.

senses.definition: a specific sibling combination consisting of a boy and a girl, typically born consecutively in the same family

examples.x: John and Emily are a pigeon pair, with John being the older brother and Emily being the younger sister.
The Smiths were thrilled to have a pigeon pair, as they had always dreamed of having both a son and a daughter.

senses.definition: used to describe someone with whom one shares a family relation

examples.x: Amanda always puts her flesh and blood first, prioritizing the needs and well-being of her family.
David couldn't believe that his own flesh and blood would betray him like that.

senses.definition: a lecture or reprimand a wife gives to her husband in private

examples.x: Catherine gave John a curtain lecture about leaving his socks on the floor, reminding him of the importance of tidiness and cleanliness.
Peter received a curtain lecture from his wife after forgetting their anniversary.

senses.definition: someone who is regarded as shameful, embarrassing, or disgraceful within their family or group

examples.x: Alice's rebellious behavior and disregard for authority have earned her the reputation of being the black sheep of the school.
In a family of doctors and lawyers, Sarah's decision to become a musician made her the black sheep.
Jennifer's conservative views make her the black sheep among her more liberal-minded friends.
While his siblings excelled academically, John was the black sheep of the family, struggling with his studies and focusing more on his hobbies.

senses.definition: someone who closely resembles their parent, especially in terms of personality, traits, or behavior

examples.x: As a brilliant scientist, Dr. Johnson's daughter proves to be a chip off the old block, showing the same level of curiosity and dedication to scientific research.
Just like his father, Mark is a chip off the old block when it comes to his love for adventure and thrill-seeking activities.

word_list.pronunciation: kˈʌt ˌɛsbˈiː ˈɔf wɪð ɐ sˈɛnt ɔːɹ pˈɛni

senses.definition: to disinherit or withdraw financial support from a family member as a way to express disapproval or to enforce certain behaviors or decisions

examples.x: When Emma dropped out of college against her family's wishes, they decided to cut her off with a penny, hoping it would motivate her to make better choices.

senses.definition: used to refer to the youngest child of a family

examples.x: Being the Benjamin of the family, Sarah is constantly compared to her older siblings and their achievements.
Lucy, the Benjamin of the family, always receives extra attention and privileges from her parents.

senses.definition: used to refer to the passing down of traditions, knowledge, values, or skills from one generation to the next, especially from a father to his son

examples.x: In the Miller family, the talent for painting is nurtured and passed down from father to son.
Music has been a passion that is handed down from father to son in the Smith family.
